items.title: Instructure strikes deal with hackers who breached it twice
items.summary: The maker of the Canvas school software said it "reached an agreement" with the hackers, but provided no guarantees that the hackers would not release the data or keep their word.

items.title: Dessn raises $6M for its production focused design tool
items.summary: A new startup called Dessn has raised $6M to build AI-powered design tools that work directly with production codebases.

items.title: TikTok now wants to be the place you book the trip you just saw on TikTok
items.summary: TikTok is systematically converting its discovery engine into a transaction layer, which both deepens user retention and opens entirely new revenue streams for its new owners.

items.title: AI is turning connected cars into pothole-finding machines
items.summary: Fleet management company Samsara has developed an AI model to detect different kinds of potholes and gauge how fast they're deteriorating.

items.title: AI voice startup Vapi hits $500M valuation after winning Amazon Ring over 40 rivals
items.summary: Vapi says its enterprise business has grown 10-fold since early 2025 as companies shift customer support and sales calls to AI agents.

items.title: Amazon launches 30-minute delivery across the U.S.
items.summary: Amazon says its new ultra-fast delivery option brings groceries, household essentials, and other items to customers’ doors in 30 minutes.

items.title: Thinking Machines wants to build an AI that actually listens while it talks
items.summary: Right now, every AI model you've ever used works the same way. You talk, it listens. It responds, you listen. Thinking Machines is trying to change that by building a model that processes your input and generates a response at the same time, so it's more like a phone call than a text chain.

items.title: Riding an AI rally, Robinhood preps second retail venture IPO
items.summary: Robinhood files confidentially for its second venture fund, this time targeting growth and early-stage startups.

items.title: Amazon employees are "tokenmaxxing" due to pressure to use AI tools
items.summary: Workers are using an internal AI tool to automate non-essential tasks.

items.title: Once again, SpaceX has set a new record for the tallest rocket ever built
items.summary: SpaceX cleared an important milestone Monday on the road to launching a new version of Starship.

items.title: Amazon’s ultrafast 30-minute deliveries are now available in more cities
items.summary: Following a limited test run in parts of Seattle and Philadelphia that started last December, Amazon Now is officially available to millions of Amazon shoppers across the United States. items.title: Waymo recalls robotaxis for driving on flooded roads
items.summary: Waymo is recalling its autonomous driving software that allowed its vehicles to drive on flooded roads. items.summary: Spotify is celebrating its 20th anniversary by giving listeners a "fully personalized look at their entire music history." items.summary: eBay has officially rejected GameStop's offer to buy the online marketplace for $56 billion, citing concerns around "operational risks" and how the acquisition will be financed. items.title: These new Roombas are smaller and cheaper
items.summary: iRobot has announced a new line of Roombas, a year after it debuted its first lidar-based robot vacuums. items.summary: Sony has announced a new version of the wearable personal air conditioner the company first revealed in 2019 ahead of the 2020 Tokyo Olympics. items.title: Some Women Are Obsessively Testing Their Vaginas to Optimize Them
items.summary: Biohacker Bryan Johnson recently bragged about his girlfriend's “top 1%” vagina as the at-home vaginal microbiome test industry is thriving. But experts are skeptical.

items.title: Why Soccer Still Defies Statistical Analysis
items.summary: Sarah Rudd, who once ran analytics for Arsenal, made her name applying the tenets of probability theory to movements on the pitch. Even she admits not everything can be solved with data.
